LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COMPLIANCE HEARING FOR DISCOVERY RESPONSES ON DEFENDANT JOI SCHEELE'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO (1) FORM INTERROGATORIES, GENERAL; (2) FORM INTERROGATORIES, EMPLOYMENT; (3) SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; (4) REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION; AND (5) REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE MATTER IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES AS SET FORTH IN THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE CERTIFIED SHORTHAND REPORTER. THE COURT UPHOLDS THE RULING ON OCTOBER 23, 2017. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED FURTHER TO DECEMBER 4, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302/LAW AND MOTION. PLAINTIFF TO FILE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE BY END OF BUSINESS DAY ON NOVEMBER 27, 2017. COURT INDICATES IT IS INCLINED TO REDUCE SANCTIONS, BUT UPON FURTHER ARGUMENT THE ISSUE OF SANCTIONS IS ALSO CONTINUED UNTIL DECEMBER 4, 2017. PLAINTIFF HAVING HAND-DELIVERED TO DEFENDANT COUNSEL A CHECK FOR THE FULL SANCTIONS AMOUNT, COURT DIRECTS COUNSEL TO RETAIN SAID CHECK UNTIL THE DECEMBER 4 HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TALK FREE, INC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. AS TO SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST ON JUNE 2, 2017, GRANTED. TALK FREE IS ENTITLED TO HAVE THE DEFAULT SET ASIDE ON BOTH MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B) AND DOING SO COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF RESOLVING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOI SCHEELE MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO (1) FORM INTERROGATORIES, GENERAL; (2) FORM INTERROGATORIES, EMPLOYMENT; (3) SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; (4) REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION; AND (5) REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. IF CODE COMPLIANT DISCOVERY RESPONSES AT ISSUE ARE SERVED TODAY, SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,000 AS TO COUNSEL MAUREEN MCFADDEN ONLY. IF RESPONSES ARE NOT PROVIDED BY TODAY, THE COURT WILL REVISIT THE SANCTION AMOUNT AND SEE IF IT ENTAILS MORE EXPENSES ON MS. SCHEELE. THE COMPLIANCE DATE FOR DISCOVERY RESPONSES ON THE MOTION HEARD TODAY WILL BE ON NOVEMBER 9, 2017 TO BE HEARD WITH OTHER MOTIONS ON CALENDAR FOR THAT DATE. THE COURT ALSO MAKES A RULING ON PLAINTIFF'S 2 EX PARTE APPLICATIONS THAT HAVE NOT BEEN FILED. PLAINTIFF'S DISQUALIFICATION MOTION TO CORNERSTONE AND MOTION TO AMEND WILL BE HELD ON NOVEMBER 9, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. MOVING PAPERS FILED /COURTESY COPIES DELIVERED TO DEPT. 302 - OCTOBER 30, 2017, OPPOSITION - NOVEMBER 6, 2017, REPLY - NOVEMBER 7, 2017. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. LAW AND MOTION 302, COMPLIANCE HEARING FOR DISCOVERY RESPONSES ON THE MOTION TO COMPEL HEARD ON 10/23/17 IS HELD. MOVING PARTY COUNSEL, MR. BYRNE SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING THAT PLAINTIFF MR. RAWLINGS IS PRECLUDED FROM PRESENTING FURTHER EVIDENCE AT TRIAL. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. A HEARING ON MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE IN RELATED ACTION, CGC-14-540735 IS CONFIRMED FOR DECEMBER 14, 2017 AS PREVIOUSLY SET. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Vacated

Compliance Hearing For Discovery Responses On The Motion To Compel Heard On 10/23/17
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Maureen E. McFadden of the law offices of Maureen E. McFadden (510-835-5203) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, David Rawlings

Attorney, Paul J. Byrne of the law offices of Cornerstone Law Group (415-357-2094) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Joi Scheele

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The parties report that plaintiff produced supplemental responses late. Compliance is argued further. Moving party counsel, Mr. Byrne shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling that as to these discovery requests, plaintiff Mr. Rawlings is precluded from presenting any further evidence in any form at trial other than what he learns subsequent to this date either through discovery or factual investigation.

A hearing on motion to consolidate in related action, CGC-14-540735 is confirmed for December 14, 2017 as previously set. Sanctions are awarded to defendant in the amount of $3,500, payable December 7, 2017. Defendant counsel may cash a $3,000 check delivered at the previous proceeding and there will be an additional amount of $500 due.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR No. 5525, e-mail: vgross@sftc.org. (L&M302)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Maureen McFadden of the law offices of Maureen McFadden (510-835-5203) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, David Rawlings

Attorney, Paul Byrne of the law offices of Cornerstone Law Group (415-357-2094) appears as counsel for Defendant/non-opposing party, Joi Scheele

Attorney, Jason M. Skaggs of the law offices of Skaggs Faucette, LLP (650-617-3226) appears by telephone as counsel for Defendant/moving party, Talk Free, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT TALK FREE, INC'S Motion To Set Aside Default/Default Judgment/Leave To Defend, as to setting aside the default entered against on June 2, 2017 is GRANTED. Talk Free is entitled to have the default set aside on both mandatory and discretionary provisions of CCP 473(b) and doing so comports with California's strong policy of resolving cases on their merits. Request for sanctions is DENIED. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Maureen McFadden of the law offices of Maureen McFadden (510-835-5203) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party, David Rawlings

Attorney, Paul Byrne of the law offices of Cornerstone Law Group (415-357-2094) appears as counsel for Defendant/opposing party, Joi Scheele

Attorney, Jason M. Skaggs of the law offices of Skaggs Faucette, LLP (650-617-3226) appears by telephone as counsel for Defendant, Talk Free, Inc.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF, DAVID RAWLINGS' Motion To Disqualify Paul Byrne And Cornerstone Law Group is off calendar as moot since Mr. Byrne and Cornerstone Law Group are no longer counsel for defendant Talk Free, Inc.

Compliance Hearing For Discovery Responses On The Motion To Compel Heard On 10/23/17
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Continued To Dec-04-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Maureen McFadden of the law offices of Maureen McFadden (510-835-5203) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, David Rawlings

Attorney, Paul Byrne of the law offices of Cornerstone Law Group (415-357-2094) appears as counsel for Defendant/moving party, Joi Scheele

Attorney, Jason M. Skaggs of the law offices of Skaggs Faucette, LLP (650-617-3226) appears by telephone as counsel for Defendant, Talk Free, Inc.

The cause is argued intwo parts. At first calling, the matter is argued and the Court givesplaintiff counsel two options: (1) produce everything or (2) be precluded from producing further evidence at trial pertaining to this motion. The matter is passed to allow plaintiff counsel to review scope of material. Recalled, the matter is argued further and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.
Plaintiff counsel agrees to comply.

The Court upholds the ruling on October 23, 2017.

The Compliance Hearing For Discovery Responses On DEFENDANT JOI SCHEELE's Motion To Compel Responses To (1) Form Interrogatories, General; (2) Form Interrogatories, Employment; (3) Special Interrogatories; (4) Requests For Admission; And (5) Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions which was Granted On 10/23/17, is CONTINUED further to December 4, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion. Plaintiff to file supplemental response by end of business day on November 27, 2017. Court indicates it is inclined to reduce sanctions, but upon further argument the issue of sanctions is also continued until December 4, 2017. Plaintiff having hand-delivered to defendant counsel a check for the full sanctions amount, Court directs counsel to retain said check until the December 4 hearing.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To (1) Form Interrogatories, General; (2) Form Interrogatories, Employment; (3) Special Interrogatories; (4) Requests For Admission; And (5) Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maureen McFadden, Esq. (510-835-5203) for Plaintiff David Rawlings.
Paul Byrne, Esq. (415-357-2094) for Defendant JOI SCHEELE.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court calls 540735 and 549327 (Lines 1 and 9) simulataneously. Plaintiff reports 540735 is uncontested. Plaintiff contests interrogatories, sanctions. Defendant reports he has not received code complaint responses. (as fully reported on the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JOI SCHEELE Motion To Compel Responses To (1) Form Interrogatories, General; (2) Form Interrogatories, Employment; (3) Special Interrogatories; (4) Requests For Admission; And (5) Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions is granted. If code compliant discovery responses at issue are served today, sanctions will be imposed in the amount of $3,000 as to Counsel Maureen McFadden only. If responses are not provided by today, the Court will revisit the sanction amount and see if it entails more expenses on Ms. Scheele. The compliance date for discovery responses on the motion heard today will be on November 9, 2017 to be heard with other motions on calendar for that date.

The Court also makes a ruling on Plaintiff's 2 ex parte applications that have not been filed. Plaintiff's disqualification motion to Cornerstone and motion to amend will be held on November 9, 2017 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Moving papers filed /courtesy copies delivered to Dept. 302 - October 30, 2017, Opposition - November 6, 2017, Reply - November 7, 2017.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, email:casey.giselle@gmail.com, Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To (1) Form Interrogatories, General; (2) Form Interrogatories, Employment; (3) Special Interrogatories; (4) Requests For Admission; And (5) Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To (1) Form Interrogatories, General; (2) Form Interrogatories, Employment; (3) Special Interrogatories; (4) Requests For Admission; And (5) Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Continued To Oct-20-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul Byrne, Esq. of Cornerstone Law Group, Tel. 415.357.2094, for defendant and moving party;
Maureen E. McFadden, Esq. of the Law Office of Maureen E. McFadden, Tel. 510.835.5203, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

DEFENDANT JOI SCHEELE'S Motion To Compel Responses To (1) Form Interrogatories, General; (2) Form Interrogatories, Employment; (3) Special Interrogatories; (4) Requests For Admission; And (5) Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

RULING: On first call, the matter was passed to give more time for plaintiff's counsel Ms. McFadden to appear and argue, as she contested the tentative ruling in its entirety per her email to the Court. On second call, Ms. McFadden, appearing via Court Call for the ex parte calendar, withdrew her objection. Therefore, the Court adopted the tentative ruling, modifying the sanctions amount, as follows:

Defendant Joi Scheele's motion to compel responses to two sets of form interrogatories, special interrogatories, requests for admission and requests for production of documents and for monetary sanctions is continued on the court's own motion to October 20, 2017. If plaintiff David Rawlings has served verified non-evasive code-compliant responses to all of the discovery at issue in the motion by October 18, 2017, sanctions of $2000 will be imposed only against counsel for Mr. Rawlings. If plaintiff David Rawlings has not served verified non-evasive code-compliant responses to all of the discovery at issue in the motion by October 18, 2017, Mr. Rawlings will be required to provide such responses no later than October 27, 2017 and sanctions of $4000 will be imposed against both Mr. Rawlings and his counsel.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.

The Judge Pro Tem shall prepare a Report and Recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Jeffrey Wohl; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
